title: Surfaced cursor + delta staleness (announce once)
description:
FIRST: 'git merge main --no-edit' in worktree; verify scripts/kb.py has compute_status, cmd_session_start, cmd_reconcile, load/save_status_cache.

Add a gitignored 'surfaced' cursor (repo-wiki/.ingest/surfaced.json — same pattern as the ingest watermark) recording which pages have already been ANNOUNCED stale, keyed by (page, verified_against sha they were stale against). Add helpers: newly_stale(stale_list, cursor) → pages whose fresh→stale transition hasn't been surfaced; mark_surfaced(pages). A page that is re-verified (verified_against bumped) or resolved naturally drops out and can re-surface if it drifts again.

Update cmd_session_start to surface NEWLY stale pages (delta) instead of the standing count, marking them surfaced. Expose the delta for testing, e.g. 'kb.py status --new' prints only newly-stale (and marks them), while plain 'kb.py status' stays a full standing report.

This is the foundation/contract reused by the post-commit hook.

Test:
- temp wiki, page covers src/**, verified_against=BASE; change+commit src.
- 'kb.py status --new' → lists the page once; second call → nothing new (cursor dedup).
- re-verify (bump verified_against to HEAD) → page no longer stale; drift it again → re-surfaces.
- py_compile passes.
acceptance_criteria: surfaced.json cursor (gitignored); newly_stale dedup so a page is announced once per fresh→stale transition; session-start surfaces delta not standing count; re-verify/resolve clears it; py_compile passes
closed_reason: Completed: gitignored surfaced.json cursor keyed (page|verified_against); newly_stale/mark_surfaced; compute_status stale entries carry verified_against; session-start surfaces delta (announce once); kb status --new. Verified: surface-once, dedup, quiet-after-reverify, re-surface-on-fresh-drift.
